behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/resolver/checker handoff across local modules (MOCK-MISS yes), not a single pure function. No behavior text requires real network/OS/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no), no AI output dependence (LLM-DEP no), and no multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is limited to extraction/inclusion from manifest, with no real external API or agent pipeline requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency-discovery end-to-end in-process against fixture manifests (including transitive deps via lockfile/graph metadata) and assert every expected direct/transitive package is present in the vulnerability-check input set.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Real failure can occur at external API contract boundary (query encoding/range semantics/response interpretation), which mocks can hide (MOCK-HIDE yes). Behavior explicitly involves database query correctness against version ranges, so real HTTP/API interaction is the true boundary (REAL-INFRA yes). No AI dependency or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability API (or real staging endpoint with production-equivalent behavior), submit queries for same package with different version ranges and verify returned CVEs align to the exact manifest range constraints.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic within one local function/object boundary. No required module handoff failure, no real infra, no AI dependence, no multi-step agent flow. Rejected higher levels: behavior text specifies pure sort precedence rules only.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sort routine with mixed severities and tie-case CVSS values; assert exact ordered list: severity rank first, then descending CVSS within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is usually parse-edit-serialize module interaction (targeted patching vs unintended collateral edits), not just isolated comparison logic (MOCK-MISS yes). No explicit need for OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no), no AI dependence, no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text is about manifest content integrity, not environment-dependent file event/runtime quirks.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update pipeline in-process on realistic fixture manifests; assert only one dependency version differs in output (semantic and textual invariants as required), with all other dependency entries unchanged.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository PR creation; failure boundary is external VCS/HTTP/API behavior (REAL-INFRA yes). Mocks can pass while real auth/branch/diff/PR semantics fail (MOCK-HIDE yes). No AI quality dependence or agentic chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real test repository and verify PR is actually opened, then inspect changed files to confirm exactly one dependency bump and no unrelated diff.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic. No required external infra, no AI dependence, and no multi-step pipeline needed to catch this specific failure. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is strictly comparison-based classification.
behaviors.test_description: Feed version pairs into semver comparator and assert breaking=true only when major version increases per semver rules (including edge cases like pre-release/build metadata as applicable).

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at orchestration boundary between breaking-change decision, PR module invocation suppression, and output/reporting state propagation (MOCK-MISS yes). Can be caught with in-process wiring and a fake PR client; no real OS/network needed for this behavior itself (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No AI output dependence (b=no) and no multi-agent chain (c=no). Rejected System: behavior text is conditional control-flow and local output, not real repository creation.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner pipeline with input that triggers major bump; assert PR client was not called and scanner output includes the review-required flag/reason.
